title: Bridge Essay: CSBBCS at Ryerson University and the embodied cognition debate.
concept: Mind not a computer — embodied, embedded, enactive, extended cognition.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Mind as Computer Critique** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** 4E cognition — embodied, embedded, enactive, extended mind; critique of computationalism.
**Phase context:** Mind not a computer — embodied, embedded, enactive, extended cognition.

**CSBBCS at Ryerson University and the embodied cognition debate.** — Can J Exp Psychol (2015). DOI: 10.1037/cep0000061. PMID: 26010022. Introduces a series of articles in the current issue of the Canadian Journal of Experimental Psychology that focuses on embodied cognition. Embodied cognition is the role of sensorimotor processes in cognition. In organizing the Past-President's Symposium at the Canadian Society for Brain, Behaviour and Cognitive Science's annual meeting, the outgoing past-president chose embodied cognition as the focus, and invited a proponent of the embodied position as well as one of the abstract position to
